title: Safe Transforms for TTA
concept: Selecting transformations for TTA requires caution. You must only apply transforms that preserve the class meaning (like horizontal flips or center crops) and avoid aggressive changes that distort features beyond recognition.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main cost of using Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) in production?

options.text: Increased latency and compute, because the model must run a forward pass for every augmented image
It permanently corrupts the model's weights
It makes training epochs take twice as long

exercises.explain: Yes! Running multiple forward passes increases latency and compute costs.

cards.title: Preserving the Class
cards.body: When applying TTA, you are modifying a test image that you don't know the label for. If you apply a transform that alters the class (like flipping a digit '3' horizontally), the model will predict 'backwards 3' and your average score will degrade. TTA transforms must be absolutely safe.

cards.title: Safe TTA Transforms
cards.body:
Safe TTA transforms are modifications that are guaranteed not to change the class meaning of any input image. Common defaults:
1. Horizontal Flipping (mirroring).
2. Multi-scale Crops (zooming in slightly).
3. Slight Brightness shifts (simulating lighting changes).

cards.title: Squinting to see clearly
cards.body: If you are trying to read a sign in the distance, squinting (cropping/zooming) or shading your eyes (brightness shift) are safe moves that help you read. Spinning around in circles 360 degrees or looking through colored glass (extreme rotation/hue jitter) makes it harder, not easier. Stick to safe moves.

cards.title: X-Ray TTA
cards.body: For chest X-rays, horizontal flips are unsafe because organs have specific left-right positions (e.g. the heart is on the left). Mirroring the image makes the organs look reversed, which breaks medical diagnostics. Safe X-ray TTA only uses tiny zooms/brightness shifts.

cards.title: No Mixup or CutMix in TTA
cards.body: It's a myth that you should use all your training augmentations in TTA. You must never use Mixup or CutMix during TTA. Blending a test image with a random training image defeats the purpose of inference, as you are trying to classify the single test image cleanly.

left.text:
Horizontal mirror (if class-safe)
Slight scale/crops (e.g. 90% zoom)
Subtle brightness adjustments

right.text:
Vertical mirroring (upside down)
Mixup / CutMix (blending)
Aggressive shearing or color shifts

exercises.prompt: Which transformation is generally UNSAFE to include in a TTA pipeline for chest X-ray scans?

options.text: Horizontal mirroring, since organ asymmetry (heart on the left) is a key diagnostic feature

exercises.explain: Correct! Mirroring medical scans reverses left-right asymmetry, creating physically impossible medical states.

exercises.prompt: True or false: You should use Mixup during TTA to blend the test image with random training images.
exercises.explain: False. Mixup is a training-only regularizer; blending test images with random training data ruins inference accuracy.

exercises.sentence: TTA transforms must preserve the ___ meaning of the image so the model predicts the correct label.
exercises.explain: The transform must keep the class meaning intact (e.g. a car must still look like a car).

exercises.prompt: Why are aggressive rotations (like 90 degrees) usually avoided in TTA for natural scenes?

options.text: Because they create unrealistic, sideways orientations that the model rarely saw during training
Because they double the channel depth of the input
Because they bypass the convolutional layers entirely

exercises.explain: Correct! Sideways natural images are unrealistic and confuse models trained on upright data.

exercises.prompt: You are building a TTA pipeline for a car model classifier. What is the most robust set of transforms to use?

options.text: Original image, horizontal flip, and a 90% center crop resized back to target size
Original image, vertical flip, and Mixup with a random dog photo
Horizontal flip, 180-degree rotation, and HSV color space conversion
Disable all crops and only use random shearing up to 45 degrees

exercises.explain: Exactly! Flips and subtle crops are robust and class-preserving. Next: how to calculate the average prediction in TTA!
